howItWorks.intro: This dog age calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: After age 2: +4.5-7.5 years per dog year depending on size
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How accurate is the 7:1 rule?
faq.answer: The old 7:1 rule is inaccurate. Dogs mature much faster in year 1 (equivalent to 15 human years) and the rate varies by size. Large dogs age faster than small dogs.

faq.question: Why do small dogs live longer?
faq.answer: Large dogs age faster at a cellular level and are more prone to cancer and joint disease. Small breeds like Chihuahuas can live 15-20 years, while Great Danes average 7-8.

faq.question: When is my dog considered senior?
faq.answer: Small dogs: 10-12 years. Medium: 8-10. Large: 6-8. Giant: 5-6. Senior dogs need more frequent vet checkups and adjusted nutrition.

faq.question: What affects dog lifespan?
faq.answer: Breed, size, genetics, diet, exercise, veterinary care, and dental health. Mixed breeds tend to live slightly longer than purebreds on average.
